However, I must be careful. Is it possible that Y is extremely valuable and X is worthless? Even then, trading 3 Y for 10 X is usually a fair or good deal if X has any value. Or maybe the other player thinks Y is very valuable. If I reject, I risk them not offering again or offering less. But I have 3 proposals. Let's try to get a better deal. I can offer to give less Y? No, I want to give Y to get X. I want to receive more X or give less Y. I have 25 Y. Giving 3 is very low cost. I have 5 X. Getting 10 is a big boost. The trade is: Get 10 X, Lose 3 Y. Net change: +10 X, -3 Y. If I assume X and Y have similar value, this is a massive win. If Y is worth 4 X, it's break even. If Y is worth 5 X, it's a loss. Given I have a huge surplus of Y, I should probably trade some Y for X. 3 Y for 10 X seems very reasonable. I will accept this trade to secure the gain in X and reduce my surplus of Y. </reason>
